top_entities.name: Pakistan
top_entities.news_summary: OPERATION SHAABAN TOLL CONTINUES CLIMBING THROUGH JUL 16 — NO SIGN OF CONCLUSION (within window, sustained/continuing): Security forces reported additional militants killed Jul 16, with the dedicated Operation Shaaban count reaching 91 by one tally and combined operation-plus-IBO totals reported at 129 since Jul 5 by security sources, continuing the daily drumbeat of militant-death reports for a third consecutive week following the Jul 4-8 coordinated insurgent assaults that killed 42 (38 security personnel, 4 civilians). No newly-dated mass-civilian-harm event since the 07-16 cycle; casualties remain overwhelmingly militants per security-source reporting, unverified by independent monitors.

top_entities.name: Lebanon
top_entities.news_summary: ISRAELI DRONE STRIKE KILLS ONE MORE CIVILIAN (JUL 10, KAFR RUMMAN) DESPITE US-BROKERED FRAMEWORK DEAL (within window, continuing, EXTERNALLY-ATTRIBUTED): An Israeli drone struck a motorcycle in Kafr Rumman on Jul 10, killing one man, days after the US-brokered framework agreement intended to pave a phased Israeli withdrawal. This follows the Jul 6 Nabatieh strike (four civilians killed, incl. a school principal, her mother, a domestic worker and a Syrian national) already logged in the 07-16 cycle. Cumulative Israeli-attack toll since Mar 2, 2026 stands at 4,303 killed, 12,202 injured, 1.6M+ displaced. All harm remains externally inflicted by Israel, not Lebanese own-conduct.

top_entities.name: Israel
top_entities.news_summary: ISRAELI STRIKE ON GAZA FUNERAL (JUL 17) KILLS AT LEAST 7, WOUNDS 22 — CUMULATIVE POST-CEASEFIRE TOLL CONTINUES CLIMBING (within window, NEW dated incident): A local hospital reported an Israeli strike on a funeral in Gaza killed at least seven and wounded 22 on Jul 17, the most recent in a continuing pattern of ceasefire-period strikes (Jul 6: 6 killed; Jul 8: 8 killed incl. 2 children). The Gaza Government Media Office's cumulative post-ceasefire toll continues to rise from the 1,123 killed/3,616 injured figure logged 07-16. Floor sustained and reinforced by a fresh dated mass-casualty incident; cannot move lower.

top_entities.name: Democratic Republic of C
top_entities.news_summary: EBOLA TOLL RISES TO 2,124 CASES / 828 DEATHS (DATA THROUGH JUL 15-16) — FASTEST-GROWING OUTBREAK ON RECORD CONTINUES (within window, NEW dated figures): The Bundibugyo-strain outbreak reached 2,124 confirmed cases and 828 deaths as of Jul 16 reporting, up from 2,011/754 (Jul 13-14) logged in the 07-16 cycle. True case count may be double the official figure. Ituri province remains most heavily affected. Disease-attributed; near-floor arithmetic forecloses any composite move regardless of severity.

top_entities.name: Iran
top_entities.news_summary: NEW WAVE: US STRIKES SIX BRIDGES IN HORMOZGAN PROVINCE OVERNIGHT JUL 16-17 — 8 KILLED, 20 INJURED; GUTERRES CALLS CIVILIAN-INFRASTRUCTURE ATTACKS 'UNACCEPTABLE' (within window, NEW escalation, interstate/US-attributable): Iran's state news agency reported at least eight killed and 20 injured after the US struck six bridges connecting cities near Bandar Abbas overnight Jul 16-17, continuing to widen the conflict beyond the Jul 15 Greater Tunb Island assault already logged in the 07-16 cycle. The US also destroyed an Iranian port surveillance tower near Chah Bahar. UN Secretary-General Guterres publicly condemned attacks on civilian infrastructure as unacceptable. NOTE — a Jul 18-dated Al Jazeera report citing a cumulative toll of 50+ killed/500+ injured 'this month' and an Iranian claim the US-Iran MoU is 'suspended' falls OUTSIDE this scan's 2026-07-17 window cutoff and is DEFERRED to the 07-18 cycle. All harm here is interstate/US-attributable and logged for the digest, NOT scored as an Iran own-conduct debit; Iran's own record (784+ executions YTD) already prices the near-floor.

top_entities.name: Russia
top_entities.news_summary: SUMY GLIDE-BOMB STRIKE (JUL 15) AND SUSTAINED CIVILIAN-CASUALTY SURGE CONTINUE — ~170 CIVILIANS KILLED/INJURED PER DAY IN JULY (within window, sustained/continuing): The Jul 15 Sumy strike (5 killed, 30 injured) remains the most recent dated mass-casualty incident, atop June's UN-recorded record of 293 killed/1,990 injured. A UN Security Council briefing this cycle confirmed the daily average of ~170 civilian casualties across Ukraine in July, with humanitarian access in parts of Donetsk intermittently suspended and a humanitarian convoy struck by drone in Dnipro region (third such incident in two months). Floor sustained and reinforced; cannot move lower.

top_entities.name: Ukraine
top_entities.news_summary: KYIV STRIKES KILL AT LEAST 14 (WITH SEPARATE UN-DOCUMENTED INCIDENT KILLING 22, INCL. 4 CHILDREN) — SUSTAINED SURGE, HUMANITARIAN ACCESS NARROWING IN DONETSK (within window, continuing): Russian missile and drone attacks on Kyiv killed at least 14 and injured 80+, heavily damaging apartment buildings, days after a separate attack killed at least 30. UN Human Rights Monitors separately documented a strike killing at least 22 civilians and injuring 100+, including four children. Humanitarian access has been narrowing in Donetsk region amid intensifying fighting, and a humanitarian convoy was struck by drone in Dnipro region — the third such incident in two months, with attacks on humanitarian personnel/assets up 20%+ year-on-year.

top_entities.name: Sudan
top_entities.news_summary: EL OBEID SIEGE CONTINUES — HALF A MILLION TRAPPED, NEARLY A MONTH WITHOUT ELECTRICITY, FOOD PRICES UP 300% (within window, sustained/continuing): The RSF's siege of El Obeid (North Kordofan capital) continues with no resolution; OHCHR documented 15 drone strikes in three weeks killing at least 45 civilians, and warns of an impending 'catastrophe' as El Obeid is expected to be the next site of major ground clashes. The UN Fact-Finding Mission's formal finding (published Jul 8-9, now outside this cycle's 7-day fresh-event window but within the 14-day lookback) that RSF conduct in El Fasher meets genocide markers remains the operative floor-level basis, with the Mission warning the same pattern is being repeated at El Obeid. The European Parliament's 476-28 RSF terrorist-designation vote (logged 07-16 cycle) remains the most recent international-response escalation. RSF harm is non-state-actor conduct; Sudan's own floor rests independently on the state's own-conduct record and cannot move lower.

top_entities.name: Cuba
top_entities.news_summary: GRID REMAINS COLLAPSED, BLACKOUTS 20-22 HRS/DAY, HRW COUNT (700+) AND PRISONERS DEFENDERS RECORD (1,200+/1,306 AS OF JUL 9) REAFFIRMED (within window, sustained/continuing): Cuba's national grid remains in its third-in-ten-days collapse (Jul 14, ~10M without power), its fifth nationwide blackout of 2026, driven by the US oil blockade compounding an overcentralized, aging system. Human Rights Watch's count of 700+ politically imprisoned and Prisoners Defenders' record of 1,200+ (1,306 as of Jul 9, already logged 07-16) were both reaffirmed by this cycle's search, with continued pot-banging protests across the island. No new record count beyond the Jul 9 figure already priced.

top_entities.name: Nigeria
top_entities.news_summary: NO NEW DATED ESCALATION BEYOND THE ALREADY-LOGGED JULY WFP CUT — 36.2M FOOD INSECURE, 300,000+ CHILDREN AFFECTED BY NUTRITION-PROGRAM SCALE-DOWN (within window, sustained, no fresh own-conduct trigger): Search results reaffirmed the same figures logged 07-16: WFP forced to scale down northeast nutrition programs in July (300,000+ children affected), 36.2M nationally food insecure, 17M+ across nine northern states at crisis/emergency/catastrophe levels, Borno's ~10,000 in catastrophic Phase-5 conditions. WFP can support only 740,000 of 6.2M in acute need across the three worst-affected northeast states; Boko Haram's farming ban in Borno continues as a non-state-actor threat vector. Donor-funding-driven, substantially external; not a discrete new Nigerian own-conduct failure.

top_entities.name: China
top_entities.news_summary: ETHNIC UNITY LAW ENFORCEMENT CONTINUES — NO NEW DATED PRC ENFORCEMENT ACTION THIS CYCLE (within window, sustained): The Ethnic Unity and Progress Promotion Law (in force since Jul 1) continues to draw international concern over its extraterritorial reach into Taiwan and the diaspora; Inner Mongolia and Xizang (Tibet) are among regions already introducing implementation measures. No newly-dated PRC enforcement or crackdown action was identified 2026-07-03 through 2026-07-17 beyond the already-known Jul 1 law and the Tibetan self-immolation protest at UN HQ logged in the 07-16 cycle.

top_entities.name: Venezuela
top_entities.news_summary: EARTHQUAKE TOLL HOLDS NEAR PRIOR FIGURE — NO NEWER OFFICIAL COUNT SURFACED THIS CYCLE (within window, sustained): The most recent official count found this cycle (Jul 9) was 3,800+ dead, 16,700+ injured, 40,000+ missing, slightly higher than the 4,333 figure referenced in the 07-16 cycle depending on source/date of report; reconstruction cost estimates exceed $37B. Labor unions and civil society continued marches in Caracas demanding new elections and criticizing the government's earthquake response. Disaster-attributed, sub-threshold; no new own-conduct escalation identified.

top_entities.name: Tunisia
top_entities.news_summary: 400+ FORMER HEADS OF STATE AND SCHOLARS DEMAND GHANNOUCHI'S RELEASE (JUL 15 INTERNATIONAL APPEAL) — SUSTAINS, DOES NOT ESCALATE, THE APPLIED DOWNGRADE (within window, continuing, no new conviction/enforcement action): On Jul 15, more than 400 former heads of state, scholars and civil-society leaders from 30+ countries signed an international appeal demanding Ghannouchi's immediate release, citing the closed-door trial and absence of judicial independence. This is international-response commentary reinforcing, not adding to, the same 'Conspiracy 2' life+30yr conviction basis for the 34.4->23.8 downgrade already applied 2026-07-14. No new Tunisian own-conduct enforcement action identified this cycle.

top_entities.name: Afghanistan
top_entities.news_summary: JUL 17 INTERNATIONAL CALL TO END TALIBAN IMPUNITY (GLOBAL JUSTICE DAY) REFERENCES JULY ICC ARREST WARRANTS FOR SENIOR TALIBAN LEADERS (within window, dated Jul 17, moderate): Human rights organizations and civil-society bodies issued a Jul 17-dated call for accountability, referencing the International Criminal Court's arrest warrants (reported this month) for Taliban Supreme Leader Hibatullah Akhundzada and Chief Justice Abdul Hakim Haqqani on crimes-against-humanity charges tied to persecution of women, girls and LGBT people. A separate HRW hunger-crisis article is dated 2026-07-18 and is OUT OF WINDOW — DEFERRED to the 07-18 cycle.

sector_alerts.title: Iran: US strikes six bridges in Hormozgan province overnight Jul 16-17 — 8 killed, 20 injured; Guterres condemns civilian-infrastructure attacks
sector_alerts.scope: countries/iran, countries/kuwait, countries/bahrain, countries/jordan, countries/oman, countries/qatar, countries/saudi-arabia, countries/united-arab-emirates
sector_alerts.summary: Iran's state news agency reported at least eight killed and 20 injured after US strikes hit six bridges near Bandar Abbas overnight Jul 16-17, continuing the Hormuz crisis into a new phase beyond the Jul 15 Greater Tunb Island assault. The US also destroyed an Iranian port surveillance tower near Chah Bahar. UN Secretary-General Guterres publicly called attacks on civilian infrastructure 'unacceptable.' A Jul 18-dated report citing a cumulative toll of 50+ killed/500+ injured this month and an Iranian claim that the US-Iran MoU is 'suspended' falls outside this scan's window and is deferred to the 07-18 cycle.

sector_alerts.title: Sudan: El Obeid siege continues — half a million trapped, food prices up 300%, UN warns of impending 'catastrophe'
sector_alerts.summary: The RSF's siege of El Obeid continues with no resolution; the city has been without electricity for nearly a month, and OHCHR documented 15 drone strikes killing at least 45 civilians over three weeks. The UN Fact-Finding Mission's genocide determination for RSF conduct in El Fasher (published Jul 8-9, now outside this cycle's fresh-event window but within the 14-day lookback) remains the operative basis, with the Mission warning the same pattern is recurring at El Obeid.

sector_alerts.title: DRC/Uganda: Ebola toll rises to 2,124 cases / 828 deaths (DRC) as Uganda discharges last patient and begins 42-day countdown
sector_alerts.summary: DRC's Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak reached 2,124 confirmed cases and 828 deaths as of Jul 16 reporting (up from 2,011/754 Jul 13-14), continuing as the fastest-growing outbreak on record with the true toll possibly double the official count. Uganda discharged its last Ebola patient Jul 16 and began its 42-day countdown to declaring the outbreak over — a genuine positive development — though WHO says Uganda has 'gone quiet' on updates for its separately reported isolated Marburg case.

sector_alerts.title: Gaza: strike on funeral (Jul 17) kills at least 7, wounds 22 — cumulative post-ceasefire toll continues climbing
sector_alerts.summary: A local hospital reported an Israeli strike on a funeral in Gaza killed at least seven and wounded 22 on Jul 17, the latest in a continuing pattern of ceasefire-period strikes this month (Jul 6: 6 killed; Jul 8: 8 killed incl. 2 children). The Gaza Government Media Office's cumulative post-ceasefire toll continues to climb past the 1,123 killed/3,616 injured figure logged in the 07-16 cycle.

sector_alerts.title: Ukraine/Russia: Kyiv strikes kill 14-22 (multiple incidents); humanitarian access narrows in Donetsk, aid convoy struck by drone in Dnipro
sector_alerts.summary: Russian missile and drone attacks on Kyiv killed at least 14 and injured 80+ in one incident, with UN Human Rights Monitors separately documenting a strike killing at least 22 (including 4 children) and injuring 100+. Civilian casualties across Ukraine continue averaging ~170/day in July. A UN Security Council briefing confirmed humanitarian access has narrowed in parts of Donetsk region, and a humanitarian convoy was struck by drone in Dnipro region — the third such incident in two months, with attacks on humanitarian personnel/assets up 20%+ year-on-year.

sector_alerts.title: AI labs: EU AI Act enforcement began Jul 10; 2026 AI Safety Index finds all frontier labs 'moving the goalposts,' highest grade a C+ (Anthropic)
sector_alerts.summary: EU AI Act enforcement officially began Jul 10, 2026, with chatbot-disclosure requirements now live (GPAI fining powers activate Aug 2). Separately, the Future of Life Institute's 2026 AI Safety Index found the highest grade earned by any frontier lab was a C+ (Anthropic); OpenAI and Google DeepMind scored C, Meta D+, and xAI/DeepSeek/Mistral effectively failed — with evaluators finding all major labs weakening previous safety commitments. Google DeepMind's CEO separately called for a US-led global AI safety watchdog with authority to coordinate industry-wide slowdowns.

sector_alerts.title: Fortune 500 sector: July layoffs continue (13,532 jobs; Microsoft largest at 4,664); DEI disclosures remain down 65% YoY
sector_alerts.summary: In July 2026, 25 companies announced layoffs totaling 13,532 jobs, with Microsoft's 4,664-position cut the largest single reduction; Thomson Reuters announced up to 500 engineering-role cuts Jul 12. This continues the established 2026 trend (167,688 tech-sector layoffs YTD) and the Human Rights Campaign Foundation's finding that Fortune 500 DEI public disclosures fell 65% in 2026 — an ongoing sector pattern, not attributable to any single newly-dated per-entity trigger this cycle.

sector_alerts.title: West/Central Africa: 55M facing crisis-level hunger this lean season amid deepest funding cuts in a decade
sector_alerts.scope: countries/nigeria, countries/mali, countries/burkina-faso, countries/chad, countries/niger, countries/sudan
sector_alerts.summary: WFP/FAO warn that 55 million people across West and Central Africa face crisis-level hunger or worse during the June-August lean season, with food-sector funding down an estimated 59% since 2022 (lowest since 2016-17) and only about one-third of 2026 funding requirements met as of June. Sudan remains the most alarming case, with famine risk identified in 14 areas across North Darfur, South Darfur and South Kordofan through September 2026, and South Sudan's Jonglei/Upper Nile counties facing famine risk through July 2026.

Human Rights Watch's count of 700+ politically imprisoned and Prisoners Defenders' record of 1,200+ (1,306 as of Jul 9, already logged 07-16) were both reaffirmed by this cycle's search, with continued pot-banging protests across the island. No new record count beyond the Jul 9 figure already priced.
